description_long: This is a vintage biplane with a dual-wing configuration, featuring a long, narrow fuselage and a pair of fixed landing wheels beneath the lower wing. The wings are straight and parallel, with roundels on the upper surfaces, suggesting a military or historical design. The tail section includes a vertical stabilizer with a tricolor pattern and a horizontal stabilizer, forming a conventional tail. The engines are mounted between the wings, with visible propellers at the front of each nacelle. The cockpit is positioned near the front of the fuselage and appears to be open, suitable for one or two occupants. The overall finish is matte, with a brownish-yellow color dominating the exterior and some accent colors on the tail and roundels.
description: A vintage biplane with dual straight wings, fixed landing gear, and an open cockpit, finished in brown with military roundels and a tricolor tail.
description_view_0: Front-left view showing the dual wings, propeller engines, and roundels on the upper wings.
description_view_1: Rear-left view highlighting the tricolor tail, horizontal stabilizer, and the open cockpit area.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail assembly, both wings, and the symmetrical placement of the engines.
description_view_3: Front-right view focusing on the landing gear, engine nacelles, and the cockpit's forward position.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: High-wing biplane configuration with two parallel, straight wings and no visible winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Twin piston engines mounted between the wings with front-facing propellers.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers forming a standard empennage.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a small tail wheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, open cockpit located near the nose of the fuselage, likely accommodating one or two seats.
